questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red; stop if you can do so safely
Stop completely and wait for green
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should stop if it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon with eight sides, a shape reserved only for stopping.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe
Treat it as a green light
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated like a stop sign: come to a full stop, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Warn of a hazard or condition ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards such as curves, intersections, or merges ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the road mean?
choices: You may pass when it is clear
Passing is not allowed on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means you may not cross it to pass.

questions.prompt: A green arrow showing in a traffic signal means:
choices: You may turn in the direction of the arrow with the right-of-way
The arrow is broken; treat it as red
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected turn in the direction it points while opposing traffic is stopped.

questions.prompt: What does an orange-colored road sign indicate?
choices: A construction or maintenance work zone
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and maintenance zones where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign means:
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light tells drivers to:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means proceed through the intersection carefully after slowing down.

questions.prompt: Broken white lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in opposite directions
Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, and a broken white line means lane changes are allowed.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
Speed up to beat oncoming cars
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to any oncoming vehicle that is close enough to be dangerous.

questions.prompt: In Georgia, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
questions.explanation: Georgia allows a right turn on red after a full stop, unless a sign prohibits it, once you yield to pedestrians and cross traffic.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Ignore it if you have the green light
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians who are crossing in a marked or unmarked crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Have the right-of-way over circulating traffic
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right-of-way, so you must yield before entering.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal?
choices: Only as you begin the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet before a turn gives other drivers time to react to your intentions.

questions.prompt: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and stop arm extended, you must:
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and wait until the red lights stop flashing.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a driveway or private road must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road
questions.explanation: When entering a roadway from a driveway or alley, you must yield to vehicles and pedestrians already on the road.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, what is the default speed limit in an urban or residential district in Georgia?
questions.explanation: Georgia's default speed limit in any urban or residential district is 30 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is the default maximum speed limit on a rural interstate highway in Georgia?
questions.explanation: The default maximum speed limit on rural interstate highways in Georgia is 70 mph.

questions.prompt: What following technique helps you keep a safe distance behind the vehicle ahead?
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you a safe following gap by counting the seconds between when the car ahead passes a point and when you reach it.

questions.prompt: In rain, fog, or heavy traffic, you should adjust your following distance by:
choices: Increasing it for more reaction time
questions.explanation: Poor conditions reduce traction and visibility, so you should increase your following distance to allow more time to react.

questions.prompt: Georgia's basic speed rule requires you to:
choices: Drive no faster than is reasonable and safe for conditions
Match the fastest car on the road
questions.explanation: Even when below the posted limit, you must drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for current road and weather conditions.

questions.prompt: On most two-lane roads in Georgia that are not otherwise posted, the default speed limit is:
questions.explanation: Where no other limit is posted and it is not an interstate or residential area, Georgia's default limit is 55 mph.

questions.prompt: Total stopping distance is made up of:
questions.explanation: Total stopping distance combines the distance traveled during your reaction time and the distance needed to brake to a stop.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is dangerous because it:
choices: Can block the normal flow and cause crashes
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly impedes the normal flow of traffic and can be just as hazardous as speeding.

questions.prompt: The default speed limit on an urban interstate or multi-lane divided highway in Georgia is:
questions.explanation: Georgia sets a default limit of 65 mph on urban interstates and multi-lane divided highways unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Drive in the center lane only
questions.explanation: The keep-right rule means slower traffic stays in the right lane and uses the left lane only for passing.

questions.prompt: You should NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: The road is straight and clear
Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
On a multi-lane highway with a passing lane
The vehicle ahead is going slowly on an open road
questions.explanation: Passing near hills or curves is unsafe because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time to return safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Check mirrors, signal, and glance over your shoulder for blind spots
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show blind spots, so you must also signal and look over your shoulder before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: You generally may not pass within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing because of the danger of crossing traffic.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
You are even with its front bumper
The other driver waves you over
questions.explanation: Returning to your lane after you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you leave it enough room.

questions.prompt: Passing on the right is generally allowed only when:
choices: The vehicle ahead is turning left and there is room
The road has a solid yellow line
questions.explanation: You may pass on the right when the vehicle ahead is making a left turn and there is enough safe room to do so.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow centerline means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates that passing is permitted when the way ahead is clear and safe.

questions.prompt: On highways, the left lane is generally intended for:
questions.explanation: The left lane is meant for passing, so slower traffic should keep to the right.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Georgia, you must leave a safe distance of at least:
questions.explanation: Georgia law requires drivers to give bicyclists at least three feet of clearance when passing.

questions.prompt: Georgia's Move Over Law requires you to do what when an emergency vehicle is stopped on the shoulder with flashing lights?
choices: Move over one lane, or slow down if you cannot
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law requires you to change lanes away from a stopped emergency vehicle, or slow down if moving over is unsafe.

questions.prompt: Large trucks are dangerous to follow closely because they:
choices: Have large blind spots and take longer to stop
questions.explanation: Trucks have big blind spots and need much greater stopping distance, so you should give them extra room.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles easy to miss in traffic?
choices: Their small size makes them hard to see and judge
They always travel in the left lane
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small profile makes it easy to overlook and hard to judge for speed and distance.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a truck driver in the truck's side mirrors, it usually means:
choices: The driver can still see you
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should:
choices: Yield and give them extra time and space
Speed up to clear the area
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a person who is blind or visually impaired, and drivers must yield the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making a right turn often:
choices: Turn from the far left lane and swing wide
questions.explanation: Trucks need extra room and often swing wide to complete a right turn, so never try to squeeze between a turning truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should treat them as:
choices: An obstacle to pass at any cost
A legal road user with the same rights and duties as drivers
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same rights and responsibilities as other drivers and must be respected on the road.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers age 21 and older in Georgia?
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent in Georgia.

questions.prompt: Under Georgia's zero-tolerance law, the BAC limit for drivers under 21 is:
questions.explanation: Georgia's zero-tolerance policy sets a 0.02 percent BAC limit for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Under Georgia's Hands-Free Law, while driving you may not:
choices: Hold or support a phone with any part of your body
questions.explanation: The Hands-Free Law prohibits physically holding or supporting a phone while driving, though hands-free use is allowed.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that will reduce a person's blood alcohol concentration?
questions.explanation: Only the passage of time removes alcohol from the body; coffee, food, and showers do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: The legal BAC limit for commercial drivers in Georgia is:
questions.explanation: Commercial vehicle operators are held to a stricter 0.04 percent BAC limit in Georgia.

questions.prompt: How can alcohol affect your ability to drive?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time, reduces coordination, and impairs judgment, all of which make driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: Prescription and over-the-counter medications can:
questions.explanation: Many legal medicines cause drowsiness or impairment, so always check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a form of distracted driving?
choices: Reading a text message while driving
questions.explanation: Reading or sending text messages takes your eyes and attention off the road and is illegal in Georgia.

questions.prompt: If you are too impaired to drive safely, the best choice is to:
choices: Use a designated driver, taxi, or rideshare
questions.explanation: When impaired, the only safe option is to let someone sober drive or to use another form of transportation.

questions.prompt: In Georgia, you must turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: Only when it is fully dark
Your windshield wipers are on due to rain
questions.explanation: Georgia law requires headlights whenever rain forces you to use your wipers, in addition to nighttime use.

questions.prompt: Georgia requires headlights from:
choices: 30 minutes after sunset to 30 minutes before sunrise
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from a half-hour after sunset until a half-hour before sunrise, plus whenever visibility is poor.

questions.prompt: In heavy fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams in fog.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane on a wet road, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and keep the wheel steady
questions.explanation: During hydroplaning, easing off the accelerator and holding the wheel steady helps the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: Headlights are required any time you cannot clearly see persons and vehicles at a distance of:
questions.explanation: Georgia requires headlights whenever you cannot clearly see at least 500 feet ahead.

questions.prompt: When the road is wet, you should:
choices: Drive faster to get through it
Slow down and increase your following distance
questions.explanation: Wet roads reduce traction, so slowing down and leaving more space gives you time to stop safely.

questions.prompt: If you go into a skid, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Take your hands off the wheel
questions.explanation: Steering gently in the direction you want to go, without slamming the brakes, helps you recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: If fog becomes so thick you cannot see, the safest action is to:
choices: Speed up to get out of it
Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Turn on high beams and continue
questions.explanation: When fog makes driving unsafe, pull well off the road, turn on your hazards, and wait for it to clear.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: In Georgia, you may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so that firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: Children under the age of 8 in Georgia must be:
choices: Secured in an appropriate car seat or booster seat
questions.explanation: Georgia law requires children under 8 to ride in a car seat or booster appropriate for their height and weight.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the car rolls back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an illegal place to park?
questions.explanation: Parking on a sidewalk or crosswalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: After parking on any hill, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving the car in park or in gear keeps it from rolling away.

questions.prompt: In Georgia, the driver and front-seat passengers are required to:
choices: Wear seat belts only on highways
Wear seat belts only at night
questions.explanation: Georgia law requires the driver and front-seat occupants to wear seat belts whenever the vehicle is in motion.

questions.prompt: When parking on a road with no curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road
Toward the edge of the road
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning the wheels toward the edge of the road keeps the car from rolling into traffic if it moves.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a Class CP instructional (learner's) permit in Georgia?
questions.explanation: Georgia allows applicants to obtain a Class CP learner's permit beginning at age 15.

questions.prompt: While driving with a Class CP learner's permit, you must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.explanation: A learner's permit holder must always have a licensed driver at least 21 years old seated in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: Under Joshua's Law, how many hours of supervised driving must a teen complete before getting a Class D license?
questions.explanation: Joshua's Law requires 40 hours of supervised driving practice, including at least 6 hours at night.

questions.prompt: During the first 6 months with a Class D intermediate license, who may ride with the teen driver?
questions.explanation: For the first six months of a Class D license, only immediate family members may ride as passengers.

questions.prompt: A Class D intermediate license holder may NOT drive between the hours of:
questions.explanation: Georgia prohibits Class D license holders from driving between 12:00 midnight and 5:00 a.m.

questions.prompt: To pass each section of the Georgia knowledge exam, you must score at least:
questions.explanation: Each section of the Georgia knowledge test has 20 questions, and you must answer at least 15 correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: After one year with a Class D license, the teen may carry no more than how many passengers under 21 who are not family?
questions.explanation: After one year, a Class D license holder may carry up to three passengers under 21 who are not immediate family.

questions.prompt: Which documents should you carry whenever you drive in Georgia?
choices: License, proof of insurance, and registration
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry a valid license, proof of insurance, and vehicle registration and show them when asked.

questions.prompt: In Georgia, accumulating how many points within 24 months results in license suspension for an adult driver?
questions.explanation: An adult driver who accumulates 15 points within a 24-month period has their license suspended.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test under Georgia's implied consent law results in:
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a chemical test for alcohol or drugs leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: A driver under 18 in Georgia can have their license suspended for accumulating how many points in 12 months?
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 face suspension for accumulating 4 or more points within any 12-month period.

questions.prompt: If you are pulled over by a police officer, you should:
choices: Get out and approach the officer
Pull over safely, stay in your vehicle, and keep your hands visible
Keep driving to a police station
questions.explanation: Pulling over safely, staying in the car, and keeping your hands visible keeps both you and the officer safe.

questions.prompt: A first conviction under Georgia's Hands-Free Law carries a fine of:
questions.explanation: A first Hands-Free Law conviction results in a $50 fine and one point on the driver's record.

questions.prompt: Violating Georgia's Move Over Law can result in a fine of up to:
questions.explanation: Failing to obey the Move Over Law can bring a fine of up to $500 in Georgia.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is considered a serious traffic violation in Georgia?
questions.explanation: Reckless driving and racing are major violations that can lead to heavy fines, points, and license suspension.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Georgia can result in:
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail time
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction carries serious penalties including license suspension, fines, and the possibility of jail time.
